def: s.m. A boy's game (one boy, after drawing several lines upon a stone, hides it; and the others, crying out kilkil-kāṅṭā, run about searching for it; whoever finds the stone has a right to strike the boy who hid it three times with his fingers on the back of the hand).

v.t. To bind, fasten; to pin, to nail; to rivet; to close, close up (as with a pin, e.g. muṅh kīlnā, 'to close the mouth, to silence'); — to drive nails into (the floor of a house, &c.) in order to avert the evil influence of Jinn, &c.; to rivet with spells, to make spell-bound, to charm, conjure, breathe a spell or incantation over; to stop the progress or the propagation of, to destroy by magic
more: v. a. 1. To pin or nail together. Ādhā bājā, ādhā gīlā, sabhoṅ ko le ek hī meṅ kīlā . Conundrum. Ans. Daftar. ( Daf a drum, tar wet. Daf-tar . An Office). 2. To conjure; charm. 1. Iskā muṅh kīl do! 2. Faqīr kī zabān kis ne kīlī hai? Prov. Who can stop the medicants tongue? 3. To destroy; stop the propagation of by magic. Chīṅṭiyāṅ kīl dīṅ . 4. To shut up; silence. Muṅh kīlnā .
v. a. To charm a snake so as to prevent his biting.
v. (dakh.) To grunt, to moan ( Harris ).

May worms get in your brain, your bones just rot away As you my love afflict, bound 'neath your magic's sway. kiṛ-khāyā, kiṛ-khāī , adj. Pitted with small-pox.

The conjunction of the two auspicious planets, Jupiter and Venus. sāhib-i-qirān , n. m. 1. One born under an auspicious conjunction of two or more planets. 2. A title of honor. sāhib-qirān , adj. Fortunate; prosperous.

(from قرن) s. m. 1. Conjunction of the planets. 2. Propinquity, contiguity. قران کرنا ḳirān karnā, To accomplish something wonderful, to perform wonders. قران السعدين ḳirānu-s-saﻌdain, The conjunction of the two fortunate planets Jupiter and Venus. ¶ ḳurʼān (from قرأ) s. m. The book containing the precepts, &c. of Muḥammad (Coran or Alcoran). قران اٿهـانا ḳurʼān uṭhānā, To swear. قران پرهاتهہ دهرنا ḳurʼān par hāth dharnā, To swear. قران خوان ḳurʼān-ḵẖẉān, A reader of the Coran; a person deposed from rule or dignity. قران کا جامه پـﮩنا ḳurʼān kā jāma pahannā, To swear excessively.
subs: s.f. The conjunction of the two fortunate planets Jupiter and Venus
To accomplish something wonderful, to perform wonders

Making, acting, doing, to make, to do; cause, instrument.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit किरण (kiraṇa). First attested in c. 1518 as Middle Hindi کرن (krn /⁠kiran⁠/).

The custom prevails among the Jāṭs, Gūjars, Ahīrs , and other inferior tribes, etc. as it did amongst the Jews. Biyāh na karāo, jhūṭ mūṭ kā chāo . Rus. Prov. Nor wife nor mistress he will make, but love-making all for love's sake. 2. Concubinage. karāo k. , v. a. 1. To marry a widow. 2. To keep a mistress. 3. To keep a man (to live with).

def: s.m. Hire, fare, rent, freight
more: n. m. Hire ( bhāṛā ). Roz phir ātī hai launḍī merī jā-kar khālī, Bhāṛ meṅ jāë kirāyā! voh kareṅ ghar khālī . Wom. Jān Sāhab. My slave girl empty-handed comes back every day, Fire burn the rent! let him just leave my house I say. kirāyā ughānā , v. a. To collect the rents, etc. kirāë chalānā, yā denā , v. a. To let; hire out.

def: s.m. The Hindu god-man Kr̤ishṇ.
more: n. prop. The most celebrated form of Vish̤ṇu or rather Vish̤ṇu himself; being distinct from the ten avatārs or incarnations. He was the younger brother of the third Rāma (or Balarāma ). His parents were Vasudeva and Devakī; but he was brought up in the house of Nanda and Yasodā, to conceal him from the tyrant Kansa, to whom it had been predicted that a child, the eighth of this family, would destroy him. In the family of Nanda he passed his youth, among the gopas and gopīs. During his childhood he vanquished the serpent Kāliya, and slew many giants and monsters. Afterward, he put the tyrant Kansa to death, protected Yudhish̤ṭhira, and kindled the war described in the Mahābhārata. He is the Apollo of the Hindūs, and is supposed by Colonel Wilford to have lived about 1300 years before Christ.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit कृष्ण (kṛṣṇa). Doublet of کرشن (kirśn) and کنھیا (kanhaiyā).

def: s.f. Dress, apparel, robe, habit; appearance; (mel.) figure, form; manner.
more: n. f. 1. A barber's or surgeon's case. 2. The piece of leather worn by a bhishtī or waterman on his left hip, on which he rests his masak or water bag.
(from کسا Heb. To cover, to clothe) s. f. 1. Dress, appearance, robe, habit. 2. ( met. ) Figure, form, manner.

def: s.m.(f. ?), Sediment, dregs, dirt; dregs of oil (in a lamp); dregs collected in a huqqā-snake.
more: s. m. 1. An insect, worm. 2. The dregs of oil in a lamp, or that collected in a ḥuḳḳa snake.

def: s.m. A worm, maggot, insect, reptile (see kīṛā).
adj. & adv. (dialec.) = kittā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), How much? what quantity? — kitte, how many (see kitnā).
more: Sant. adv. S. कुत्र Where; whither. Chhān binā ab kit maiṅ baiṭhūṅ ? Rus. No cover o'er my head, where can I sit?

def: s.m. Cutting; a section, &c.

ety: for A. قطعة qit̤ʻat, n. of un. fr. قطع; see qat̤ʻ
def: s.m. A cutting, segment, section, division; a fragment, piece, scrap; a detached portion; a patch or plot (of ground); — a strophe; — distichs corresponding in measure and rhyme, but without a mat̤laʻ
more: n. m. 1. A detached portion; scrap; fragment; piece. Āpkā ek qatٴa khat āyā . 2. A patch or plot of ground; a tract. 3. A kind of verse, in which the ever. lines rhyme and the meaning of the first verse of each stanza is completed in the last. qataī, qatan , adv. Completely; finally; absolutely; positively; peremptorily; once for all.

s. m. 1. A section, a cutting, a fragment, a division. 2. A strophe. قطعه بند ḳit̤ʼaﻌ-band, adj. A kind of verse, in which the meaning of the first verse of each stanza is completed in the last. ¶ ḳat̤ʼﻌ or ḳut̤ʼaﻌ, A division, plot, parcel or portion of land, segment, portion; cut, shape.
subs: s.m. A kind of verse (consisting of pairs of baits or couplets) in which the meaning of the first bait is incomplete without the bait immediately following (or in which the meaning of the first verse of each stanza is completed in the last).

ety: imperf. part. of kīnā = karnā, qq.v.
def: part. Doing, making ( = kartā).
s.f. A book; a writing, despatches; scripture; a letter
more: n. m. pl. kutab , کتب wrote. A book kitāb-i-ilahī, kitāb-i-āsmānī, kalām majīd , n. m. The Quran. kitāb pe chaṛhānā , v. a. To register; put down; make a note of.

(from کتب Heb. To write) s. f. A book, writing, despatches, scripture, a letter. کتاب الهي kitāb-i-ilāhī, The holy book, holy writ. کتابخانه kitāb-ḵẖāna, A library. کتاب رو kitāb-rū, adj. Oval or long-faced. کتاب فروش kitāb-farosh, A bookseller.
subs: or kitāb-ě-āsmāni, or kitāb-ě-majīd, The holy book, holy writ, the Qorʼān
(-ko), To enter in a book, to book, register, put down, make a note of
s.m. (f. -ī), One who has a revealed Book, i.e. a Jew, a Christian, or a Moḥammadan (syn. ahl-ě-kitāb); — a vendor or a maker of books.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کِتَاب (kitāb), borrowed from Arabic كِتَاب (kitāb). Displaced Old Hindi कतेब (kateba), borrowed from the same term. Compare Punjabi کتاب (kitāb), Sindhi ڪِتابُ (kitābu).

ety: for A. کتابة kitābat, inf. n. of کتب 'to write'
def: s.f. The profession of a copyist.
(for A. کتبة kitabat, pl. of kitābat), s.m. pl. Writings; inscriptions; epitaphs, &c.
more: s. m. plur. (of کتابت) Inscriptions, writings.

ety: for A. کتابة kitābat, inf. n. of کتب 'to write'
def: s.f. The profession of a copyist.
more: n. f. The profession of a copyist. kitāb-rū, yā kitābī chehrah , n. m. A long face.

def: direct plural of کِتاب (kitāb)
Book of Aqdas
Iqan
the Bible
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کِتابِ مُقَدَّس (kitāb-i muqaddas), from Arabic الْكِتَابُ الْمُقَدَّس (al-kitābu l-muqaddas, literally “the holy scripture”).

def: adj. Of a book, relating to a book or writing; in possession of a revealed Book; — like a book, oblong; — s.m. One who is (or a people who are) in possession of a revealed Book, i.e. a Jew, a Christian, or a Muslim
more: adj. Relating to a book or writing. The phrase is applied, by Mahomedans, to Jews and Christians, as in possession of a revelation or scriptural authority from the same Deity, although, according to some of them, corrupted or superseded. kitābī ādmī , A bookish man. kitābī-ilm , n. m. Book-learning or knowledge.
adj. Of a book, like a book oblong. کتابي چـہره kitāb-ī chihra, A long or oval face.
subs: s.m. A long or oval face

def: s.f. (prov.) A small book.
more: (dimin. of کتاب) s. f. A small book.

def: vocative plural of کتاب
oblique plural of کِتاب (kitāb)
(for ke-taʼīṅ, qq.v.), postpn. To, up to; for; on, at (often used as equivalent to ko, the postpn. of the dative and acc. cases).
more: (or dakh. kitaiṅ or kataiṅ for کي تئين) postpos. To, up to, for, on, at, or denoting the objective case.

def: adj. (prov.) = kitek, and kittā, qq.v. adj. (f. -ī), How much? what quantity? — kitte, how many (see kitnā).
more: n. m. S. कटक, Pr. kadaa . A large army.
adj. (dakh.) Some, how much? see کتنا kitnā.

def: To suffer distress, to twist or twine, to be very uneasy.

ety: inf. n. of قاتل qātala, 'to fight with,' iii of قتل 'to kill'
def: s.f. Fighting, battle, slaughter.
more: (from قتل) s. f. Battle, slaughter, fighting. ¶ ḳattāl, adj. Great slayer, killer, murderer.

def: s.m. A guitar.
s.f. A string (of camels, &c.); a string, line, row, series, order, rank, range, train, &c.
more: n. f. قطر dropping of water, etc., tying camels in a line. See panktī . Row; string; range; train; procession; file; rank and file ( silsilah , 1). Ūṅṭoṅ kī qatār. qatār bāṅdhnā , v. a. To string together; place, set, or form in order; arrange in a series; range; marshal. qatār meṅ , adv. In a line; in succession ( silsilevār ).
To bind, to tie together; which meaning is preserved in II. conj. of قطر) s. f. A line, string (of animals), rank, order, series, row, range, file.
subs: (-kī), To string together; to put or place in a line or row, or in order; to arrange in a series; to marshal
adv. In a line, or series; in succession.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic قِطَار (qiṭār).

def: adj. & adv. (rustic) = kittā; and kaʼī-ek, qq.v. adj. (f. -ī), How much? what quantity? — kitte, how many (see kitnā).
s.f. Fame, &c. (see kīrti).
(dakh.) Tick (small noise). کٿ کٿ کرنا kaṭ-kaṭ karnā (dakh.) To click ( Harris ).

v. a. 1. To gnash or grind (the teeth). 2. To urge cattle to go faster; to cry gee wo. Arai! ṭuk ek baldān ne kiṭkiṭā jo dinghāveṅ! Rus. I say! stir up your cattle a little and make them step out! kiṭkiṭ'ī , n. f. Grinding one teeth.
s.m. Concealing; concealment.
s.m. The thin pellicle on a date-stone; — name of the dog which accompanied the seven sleepers
more: s. m. 1. The thin pellicle on a date stone. 2. Name of the dog which accompanied the seven sleepers. نقيروقطمير naḳīr o ḳit̤mīr, adv. Minutely, exactly, to the point.
subs: adj. & adv. Minute, particular, exact; — minutely, exactly, to the point.

ety: Prk. केत्तिलओ; S. कियतिकः; see kittā
def: adj. & adv. (f. -ī), How much? what quantity? (kitne) how many? — as many as; several; some
more: adv. S. कति or कियत Pr. keddahā, kettia . 1. ( kuttān ) How many; what number; how much. 2. As many as; several. 1. Baṛe baṛe bahe jāëṅ, gadhā kahe kitnā pānī? Prov. Great ones away swept down a heap, the donkey asks 'is the water deep?' (A fool would dare where better men ill fare). 2. Chuṛyāṅ chubkī-dār haiṅ, chahiyeṅ tumheṅ kitek, Yahāṅ ān-ke pahan jā adhik ek se ek . Play. Chaubolā. Lac-bangles shining ho! as many as you want, Come try them—one and all they're superexcellent. kitnā hī . However; howsoever; howmuchsoever.
How much? how many? some, several: see کتا kittā. کتني ايک kitne ek, Some, some few. کتناهي kitnā-hī, Howsoever, howsoever much or many.
subs: adv. Howsoever, however much or many, how-much-soever.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀺𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀬 (kittiya) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀦- (-na-), from Sanskrit *कियत्त (*kiyatta), variant of कियत् (kíyat). Doublet of کِتَّا (kittā). Cognate with Punjabi ਕਿਤਨਾ /کِتْنا (kitnā).

def: s.m. A kind of sour dish.
adj. (f. -ī), How much? what quantity? — kitte, how many (see kitnā).
more: n. f. Bloodshed kaṭā-kaṭī . A general slaughter or massacre. kaṭai'yā ; A. qātil , n. m. One who makes a general massacre. adh-kaṭā , adj. Half-cut.

adj. 1. Hard; strong; robust; able-bodied. Haṭtā kaṭṭā hai . 2. Mortal; deadly. Kaṭṭā bairī . 3. Strict; orthodox; bigoted. Kaṭṭā musalmān. kaṭṭā ; Sant. se ; Tir. pitthā , n. m. A large fat louse. Paṛe sīs māṅ seroṅ kaṭṭe, phūhaṛ samjhe chaṭṭe baṭṭe . Rus. Fat lice full many seers' weight on her head, between the finger nails crack crack they're dead, the ninny hears but rattle sounds instead.
How many? how much? as many, some.
ex: میرَا کُتَّا ہَمیشَہ بِلِّی پَر بَھوںکْتَا ہَےْ۔
merā kuttā hameśa billī par bha͠uktā hai.
My dog always barks at a cat.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀢 (kutta).

def: s.m. Secretion; excretion; excrement; dirt, filth, impurity
s.f. (old H.), corr. of kīrtti, q.v. s.f. Mention, report; good report, praise; fame, renown, glory; — a proper name
subs: To make a name, to acquire renown
(f. -matī), or kīrtti-vān, or kīrtti-vant (f. -vatī), adj. & s.m. Praised, celebrated; famous, renowned, glorious; — a famous person, &c.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit कति (kati), ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *kʷóti.

def: s.f. = kiwāṅć, q.v. s.f. Cowach, Mucuna prurita; or Dolichos pruriens.

ety: v.n. fr. قوم; see qawām, and qiyām
def: s.m. That on which anything rests, or in which it consists; substratum; essence, substance; competency; consistency; syrup; materials, ingredients, requisites.
more: n. m. 1. See chāshnī , 1. 2. Materials; ingredients; requisites.

def: adj. Syrup-like, syrupy; thick, substantial, &c.
s.f. Cowach, Mucuna prurita; or Dolichos pruriens.
s. Name of a plant, cowitch. (Dolichos pruriens).
s.m. (dialec.), See kiwāṛ.
more: n. m. S. कपाट Pr. kapaṭo . 1. A leaf of a door. Ek pahelī maiṅ kahūṅ—uṛī jāt hai Lankā, Ādhā nām kivāṛ hai, aur ādhā nām hai pankhā . Charade. Ans. Paṭ-bījnā . A glow-worm. A riddle hear—far off Ceylon its flight can span, Its first half is a door, its second is a fan. 2. A door. kivāṛ band k., bheṛnā, yā denā , v. a. To shut a door. kivāṛ'ī , n. f. 1. Dim of kivāṛ , q. v. 2. See pardah , 4. 3. ( chhātī ke kivāṛ ). The intercostal bones. (?)

ety: Prk. कवाडो and कवाडओ; S. कपाटः and कपाटकः
def: s.m. A door; leaf or panel of a door; shutter or fold of a door; gate (of a city), entrance.
s.m. (dialec.) = kiwāṛā, q.v.

ety: Prk. कवाडो and कवाडओ; S. कपाटः and कपाटकः
def: s.m. A door; leaf or panel of a door; shutter or fold of a door; gate (of a city), entrance.
s.f. A small door, a small gate; a wicket; — the piece which covers the chest in an angarkhā, q.v.; — an intercostal bone (?).

ety: perf. part. of karnā; and = Ap. Prk. किया and कइओ, Prk. करितओ, or कलिट्ओ = S. कर+इत+कः
def: part. (f. kiʼī, and kī), Done, performed, &c. (see karnā); — s.m. Action, deed; doings.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀺𑀁 (kiṃ), 𑀓𑀺 (ki), from Sanskrit किम् (kim, “what, which”). Related to کِہ (ki). Compare Nepali क्या (kyā).

def: leadership (ie. status of a leader); constituency (ie. the zone of one's leadership)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قیادت (qiyādat), from Arabic قِيَادَة (qiyāda).

def: (from قوف) s. m. Appearance, countenance, mien, likeness, air, manner, mode, representation.

ety: for A. قيافة qiyāfat; prob. a post-classical form fr. قوف 'to follow in the footsteps' (of
def: s.m. Appearance, semblance, likeness, resemblance; imitation, representation; countenance, mien, face, look; air, port; manner, mode; physiognomy (syn. firāsat)
more: n. m. قوف following the foot teps. Face; look (صورت 2). Qayāfe se zāhir sarāpā shaūr, Jabiṅ par barastā shujā at kā nūr . Mir Hasan. qayāfa-shinās , n. m. A physiognomist. qayāfa-shināsī , n. f. Physiognomy ( ilm-i-qayāfah ).

def: s.m. Standing upright; rising up; up-rising, insurrection; resurrection; standing position (in prayer); standing still; stagnancy; stationariness, rest; congealed state (of water, &c.); stability, permanence; stay, halt; settlement, residence
more: n. m. قوم stood. 1. Standing position in prayer. 2. Stay ( ṭairāo , 2). 3. Existence; being. 4. Durability; permanence ( sthirtāī , 1). Qayām-i-jism-i-khākī hai nafas par, Havā par hai binā apne makāṅ kī . Arshad. This earthy frame with breath must go as't came. This mausion hath no firmer base than air. qayām-i-aman , n. f. Preservation of the peace. qayām-pizīr , adj. Stationary; at rest; reposing. qayām k. , v. a. To stay; lodge ( ṭhairnā , 3—5). Āp kab tak qayām kareṅge?
(from قوم) s. m. 1. Standing erect, resurrection. 2. Stability, permanence. 3. Attention. 4. Residence, settlement.
s. m. Standing position in prayer.
subs: being; appointment (to a charge, &c.); looking after, keeping, preservation (of the peace, &c.); application, attention (to)
adj. Stationary, at rest; stable, permanent, &c.
(-meṅ), To stay, remain (at), to settle, reside, lodge; to make a halt, to halt.

ety: for A. قيامة, fr. qiyām, q.v.
def: s.f. The resurrection, the last day; — confusion, commotion, tumult, uproar, extraordinary to-do; anything extraordinary; a scene of trouble or distress; a great calamity; excess; — adj. & adv. Wonderful; excessive, very great; heavy, grievous, oppressive; — wonderfully; excessively, extremely, very
more: n. f. 1. The general resurrection; the last day. Qayāmat ke din tumhārā dāman hogā, aur merā hāth! Wom. On the judgment day my hand upon your skirt will be. (Our account will be settled then). 2. ( Met .) Calamity ( āfat , 1). 3. Weeping and wailing. 4. Commotion; tumult; uproar ( gul shor ). qayāmat , adj. 1. Very great (عاقبت 2). Tum to qayāmat jhūṭ bolte ho . Wom. 2. Heavy; oppressive ( bhārī , 12, 15, 16). Woh din hijr kā us pe shāmat huā, Use kāṭnā din qayāmat huā . Mīr Hasan. qayāmat uṭhānā, barpā k., yā machānā , v. n. To make a hell of a noise or row. qayāmat ānā , v. n. 1. To come (the resurrection). 2. To befal (a great calamity). Woh jab pahlū se uṭhte haiṅ qayāmat ā hī jātī hai . Shaidā. qayāmat k., yā toṛnā . 1. To bear hard upon. 2. To oppress.
s. f. 1. The general resurrection, the last day. 2. ( met. ) Calamity, excess. 3. Excessively great, excessive, extremely. قيامت کرنا ḳiyāmat karnā, 1. To oppress. 2. To do any thing wonderful.
subs: or qiyāmat bar-pā karnā, or qiyāmat maćānā, v.n. To raise a commotion, &c.; to make a terrible to-do, &c.
(-par), To let troubles, &c. like those of the resurrection loose (upon), to bring calamity (upon); to practise great oppression (upon), to oppress grievously
(-par), The resurrection to break (upon); a great calamity to befall
v.n. To raise a tumult, &c. ( = qiyāmat uṭhāna); — to practise oppression (upon, -par), to oppress ( = qiyāmat toṛnā); — to do something wonderful, to achieve wonders.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قِیَامَت (qiyāmat), from Arabic قِيَامَة (qiyāma).

def: s.f. Stability; steadiness.

s.f. Bed (of a garden or field); a border; a frame; a bed (of salt, e.g. non-kī kyārī); — a field or plot of ground under cultivation having a raised border, bank, or hedge round it; a garden with a fence or border round it.
more: n. f. S. केदार 1. A bed (of a garden or field). Kyārī kyoṅ na banvāī, ḍomnī kyoṅ na gavāī? Du-sukhnah. Ans. Bel na thī . Why was n't the field prepared, why did n't the ḍomnī sing? Ans. 1. I had n't a hoe. 2. I hadn't the money. 2. A frame. 3. A salt bed. Non kī kyārī .
s. f. 1. A bed (of a garden). 2. A frame.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑁂𑀆𑀭 (keāra), from Sanskrit केदार (kēdāra, “field”).

ety: inf. n. of قيس; and also inf. n. of iii of قيس 'to measure, to compare (with
def: s.m. Measuring (by or with); comparing (with); measurement, comparison; — reasoning, ratiocination; a syllogism; — regular form, analogy, rule; judgment, opinion; thought, conception; fancy; theory; supposition, conjecture, guess
more: n. m. قيس measuring, comparing See bichār , 3, 6, aṭkal, andāz , 4, and andāzāh , 1. 1. Thought; judgment; opinion. 2. Conjecture.
(from قيس) s. m. Measuring or comparing (by reason), judgment, opinion, supposition, thought, guess, theory, a syllogism. قياس کرنا ḳiyās karnā, To guess, think, suppose.
subs: v.t. To think, judge, opine, conceive, imagine, suppose, guess; to estimate

def: adv. By analogy; by conjecture or guess, conjecturally.
more: adv. By guess ( aṭkal se ). qayās se bāhar . Beyond conception or calculation. qayās k., yā lagānā , v. a. To guess ( aṭkalnā , 1). qayās meṅ ānā , v. n. To be conceivable.

ety: for A. کياسة, inf. n. of کيس 'to be quick,' &c.
def: s.f. Quickness of parts; vivacity; ingenuity, sagacity; shrewdness; cunning
more: (from کاس) s. f. Ingenuity, quickness of parts (or great vivacity), cunning. صاحب کياست ṣaḥib-kiyāsat, Ingenious, sagacious.
